rulings.Case Title: THOMAS B HOLTON VS. R SCOTT DERVAES II et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Amend And Correct Memo Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 25, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF THOMAS HOLTON's Motion To Amend And Correct Memorandum Of Costs. Granted. No opposition filed and for good cause shown. (CCP § 473(a)(1).) Plaintiff's counsel admits his mistake in filing the previous Memorandum of Costs After Judgment. (Auerbach Decl., 7.) Plaintiff is permitted to file a Memorandum of Costs and Credits (Form MC-012) correcting the acknowledgment of total credit to date (including returns on levy process and direct payments) from the amount of $ 6,600.00 to $ 5,800.00. (See Jud. Council Form MC-012, 4.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PHILADELPHIA INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. RICHARD GREENLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Johannes Huwyler'S Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To 1) Form Interrogatories 2) Req. For Production Of Documents 3) For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 25, 2013, Line 5, Defendant Johannes Huwyler'S Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To 1) Form Interrogatories 2) Req. For Production Of Documents 3) For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. No opposition filed. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $1,305 plus $90 in filing fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: ALLIED HEATING & AIR CONDITIONING, INC., A VS. GOTHAM BAY CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 25, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT GOTHAM BAY CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION's application FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Denied as to application for right to attach order as to Brando Empire San Francisco LLC. Plaintiff has not met its burden of establishing that the amount sought by Gotham Bay Construction Corporation ("GBCC") ($686,404.53, including costs of $20,000 and estimated attorney fees of $250,000) is fixed or readily ascertainable. CCP §483.010. GBCC based these amounts on the terms of an unsigned agreement. (Garay Decl. 4, 19-22 and Ex. A.) Even if there was an oral agreement to do some work, the amount is not fixed or readily ascertainable. The quantum meruit claim was raised for the first time in reply. Plaintiff has not shown that the amount sought in quantum meruit recovery is fixed or readily ascertainable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROSARIO M. O'CONNOR et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 25, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANTs WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., WELLS FARGO & COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the common law conversion and UCC 3420 causes of action are sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiffs have leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege that they were the payees on the cashier's checks. See Lewis v. Telephone Employees Credit Union (9th Cir. 1996) 87 F.3d 1537, 1552. The demurrer to the negligence cause of action is overruled. The negligence cause of action incorporates numerous instances of negligence and Defendant fails to show that the entire cause of action ins preempted. The demurrer to the negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Negligent infliction of emotional distress is not a distinct cause of action and emotional distress damages are not recoverable in this case. See Bro v. Glaser (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 1398. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DONNY CHU CONSTRUCTION INC. DBA DC CONSTRUCTION VS. RICHARD CROCKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 25, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANTs RICHARD CROCKER, RC HORIZON LLC, DARREN HODIES & DARREN ROSS HODIES CONSTRUCTION COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrers to the 11th cause of action for defamation/libel and 12th cause of action for slander are overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads the substance of the defamation and that the statements were published to third parties. The demurrer to the 13th cause of action for fraud is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff cannot allege that it relied on the defendants' statements. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: ZYNGA INC. VS. ALAN PATMORE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Alan Patmore'S Motion For Protective Order Pursuant To Ccp Sec 2019.210
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 25, 2013, Line 6, Defendant Alan Patmore'S Motion For Protective Order Pursuant To Ccp Sec 2019.210 Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is denied. Zynga has described its trade secrets with reasonable particularity so as to permit Defendants to ascertain the boundaries within which the secrets lie. See, Brescia v. Angelin (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 133, 144. Any reasonable doubt regarding the adequacy of the description is resolved in favor of allowing discovery to proceed. Id. at 145. Mr. Patmore shall respond to Plaintiff's First Sets of: Form Interrogatories; Special Interrogatories; Requests for Production of Documents; and Requests for Admissions within 30 calendar days of the date of this order. The objections to evidence and to declarations are denied as moot. The Court did not rely on such evidence in deciding the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm
